label: commit: research
prompt:
You are running the commit flow for ponder 'research' in the sdlc workspace.

The ponder status has already been marked committed. Your job is to synthesize
the idea into milestones and features.

## Step 1 — Load context

```bash
sdlc ponder show research
sdlc ponder artifacts research
sdlc ponder session list research
```

Read every scrapbook artifact and all session logs (`sdlc ponder session read research <N>`) to understand the full idea. Also check existing milestones to avoid duplication:
```bash
sdlc milestone list
sdlc feature list
```

## Step 2 — Size the idea

Based on scope:
- Single concern → one feature, add to an existing milestone if one fits
- Medium idea → one new milestone with 2–5 features
- Large idea → multiple milestones

## Step 3 — Create milestones and features

```bash
sdlc milestone create <slug> --title "<title>" --vision "<one-line why>"
sdlc feature create <slug> --title "<title>"
sdlc milestone add-feature <milestone-slug> <feature-slug>
```

Track every milestone slug you create or update.

## Step 4 — Link committed-to milestones

Record which milestones came from this ponder:
```bash
sdlc ponder update research --committed-to <milestone-slug> [--committed-to <milestone-2> ...]
```

## Step 5 — Report

Output a brief summary: what was created, which milestones, suggested next command.
